creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
Comment on attachment 179157
patch-chromium-use-system-ffmpeg

Thanks. The patch looks mostly fine. I've only checked runtime briefly in 10.3 i386 jail via Youtube + h264ify = no apparent issues.

> -LIB_DEPENDS=	libcairo.so:graphics/cairo \
> +LIB_DEPENDS=	libavcodec.so:multimedia/ffmpeg \
> +		libcairo.so:graphics/cairo \

_DEPENDS are usually sorted by port origin, not by provided file. www/chromium has inconsistent sorting, so I'm not sure which style it wants to adhere.

> +--- media/ffmpeg/ffmpeg_common.h.orig	2017-01-20 16:39:21.594594000 +0000
> ++++ media/ffmpeg/ffmpeg_common.h	2017-01-20 16:40:36.609384000 +0000

Regen via "make makepatch" to avoid portlint complaining.

> +-#include "third_party/ffmpeg/libavcodec/avcodec.h"
> +-#include "third_party/ffmpeg/libavformat/avformat.h"
> +-#include "third_party/ffmpeg/libavutil/imgutils.h"
> ++#include <libavcodec/avcodec.h>
> ++#include <libavformat/avformat.h>
> ++#include <libavutil/imgutils.h>

I wonder why ArchLinux or Gentoo didn't hit this. Maybe because

  [4206/23993] python ../../tools/generate_shim_headers/generate_shim_headers.py --generate --headers-root /wrkdirs/usr/ports/www/chromium/work/chromium-55.0.2883.87/third_party/ffmpeg --output-directory /wrkdirs/usr/ports/www/chromium/work/chromium-55.0.2883.87/out/Release/gen/shim_headers/ffmpeg_shim libavcodec/avcodec.h libavformat/avformat.h libavutil/imgutils.h

  $ cat $(make -V WRKSRC)/out/Release/gen/shim_headers/ffmpeg_shim/third_party/ffmpeg/libavcodec/avcodec.h
  #if defined(OFFICIAL_BUILD)
  #error shim headers must not be used in official builds!
  #endif
  #include <libavcodec/avcodec.h>
